topic: Tracking 80 leave balances in a shared spreadsheet — someone always gets the wrong number
format: Write a numbered list of 4-5 warning signs — make them specific enough that most readers recognise at least three of them
post:
You recognise at least three of these. Probably four.

1. Someone asks for their leave balance — you open a spreadsheet, scroll through 80 rows, and still aren't sure the number is right.
2. Two managers edited the file on the same day. Now there are two versions and neither is dated.
3. An employee took 3 days in March. It was never updated. You found out during their annual review.
4. A staff member disputed their balance. You couldn't prove the correct figure. You gave in.
5. Someone is on leave right now and you're not 100% sure it was formally approved.

This isn't a spreadsheet problem. It's a trust problem.

When your records are unreliable, every leave conversation turns into a negotiation. HR spends time they don't have chasing numbers. Employees start doubting the process. And finance can't close payroll cleanly because nothing quite matches.

80 employees. One shared file. No audit trail. That's not a system — that's a liability waiting to surface at the worst possible moment.

SmartPro Hub tracks every balance automatically, logs every request, and keeps one version of the truth — visible to HR, managers, and employees in real time.

Starts at OMR 12/month. 14-day free trial, no credit card needed.

Which of the five hit closest to home? Drop it in the comments.
